old_claim.note: pam_recognition_constraint was previously treated as a plausible hard prerequisite for pam_specificity_engineering without a field-specific edge receipt.

new_claim.note: Engineering altered PAM specificity depends on the Cas9 PAM-recognition constraint that defines which target sites wild-type Cas9 can recognize.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and results describing altered PAM-specificity Cas9 variants designed around PAM recognition limits of wild-type Cas9.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source shows that engineering PAM specificity targets the PAM-recognition constraint that limits which DNA sites wild-type Cas9 can edit.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This is a required dependency because the engineered technology is defined by changing the PAM-recognition requirement rather than merely co-occurring with it.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: pam_specificity_engineering -> pam_recognition_constraint remains required; this receipt adds source-shaped evidence for the existing CRISPR field edge.
would_reject_if: The cited work did not discuss PAM requirements or altered PAM specificity.
The node was broadened to all nuclease-specificity engineering without changing this edge.
The graph modeled PAM recognition as a downstream application of engineered variants.
short_reason: PAM-specificity engineering directly depends on the existence of the PAM-recognition constraint it modifies.
